workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Just came out of migration architecture with Nod Publishers. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Nod Publishers
Date: 2024-02-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is migration architecture.

[00:07:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:19:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:23:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:25:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:28:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:30:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-F trap (Skips manager-decision items when consensus was reached); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-F failure mode (Skips manager-decision items when consensus was reached)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-F -- Skips manager-decision items when consensus was reached
